variation_instruction.prompt: IMPORTANT: Implement from the inside out. Use the wishful programming approach. Write the internal code you wish you had, before you write the code that uses it.

llm_gate_results.reasoning: The implementation faithfully covers all explicit requirements: (1) zinnen.js correctly implements the Dutch time-to-sentence conversion for all minute patterns from the spec table, using computed differences (30-min, 60-min) to generate 'vijf voor half', 'tien voor half', etc. for all minutes generically—which produces correct results for both the listed checkpoints and intermediate values; (2) SentenceClock.js implements the editable fill-in mode with drag-and-drop, double-click placement/removal, auto-check on completion, visual error feedback with reset, and 2-4 confounders that are not duplicates of correct words; (3) main.js adds the 'Zin' third menu option alongside existing 'Analoog', 'Digitaal', and 'Beide'; (4) store.js implements the PAIRINGS array for 'beide' mode with all three two-way pairings (Analoog↔Digitaal, Analoog↔Zin, Digitaal↔Zin) chosen randomly per round; (5) per-mode pairing logic (each editable mode gets a random reference from the other two); (6) 24-hour to 12-hour modulo conversion is handled; (7) placeholder tests exist in both test/store.test.js and test/zinnen.test.js; (8) central state store is used without external libraries; (9) CSS styles for the sentence clock and interactive word blocks are present. No significant requirement appears to be missing or materially misimplemented.

The test suite demonstrates strong quality across all files. New zinnen.test.js contains 20 meaningful tests with real assertions covering all Dutch time expression breakpoints (0, 5, 10, 15, 20, 25, 30, 35, 40, 45, 50, 55 minutes), 24-hour edge cases, and randomization verification via looping (20 iterations). The modified store.test.js adds comprehensive tests for new game modes including zin mode correctness checking with explicit assertions. Tests use property-based loops to verify randomness invariants rather than trivial single-call checks. No evidence of removed tests or weakened assertions — the coverage was expanded with new functionality.

The SentenceClock component and its CSS styles are well-integrated with the existing design system. Word slots and tray use flex-wrap to prevent overflow, the editable layout fits within the 240px min-height clock cell, responsive breakpoints handle narrow viewports, and all visual elements (colors, fonts, borders, spacing) follow the established CSS variables. No occlusion, misalignment, clipping, or readability issues detected in the code review.
